author_note: Generated from a hand-authored method template + profile-grounded scenario (deterministic, no tokens).
rubric: correct if a command is included

turns.content: quick - which port is the control channel?
UDP 5005. (Camera's TCP 5000/5001, launcher's 5010.)

author_note: Generated from a hand-authored method template + profile-grounded scenario (deterministic, no tokens).
rubric: correct if a command is included

turns.content: quick one - do I really need to keep D+/D- twisted through the splice?

Yes. Untwisting at the splice is where USB enumeration dies in this EMI bay - keep the pair twisted through the joint, strain-relieve at the epoxy exit, and wiggle-test before you pot it. Cheap discipline, expensive to skip.

author_note: Generated from a hand-authored method template + profile-grounded scenario (deterministic, no tokens).
rubric: correct if a command is included

turns.content: which build actually has the live IMU?
The OneServo target - it reads the live MPU-6050. main.cpp still runs the PID off a hardcoded-zero IMU stub; wiring the live read into the loop is the big open gap.

author_note: Generated from a hand-authored method template + profile-grounded scenario (deterministic, no tokens).
rubric: correct if a command is included

turns.content: quick one - is the pi the git box?
Yes - git rides wlan0 (internet side). The wired eth0 link to the pilot laptop stays gateway-less and never touches git or the internet.

author_note: Generated from a hand-authored method template + profile-grounded scenario (deterministic, no tokens).
rubric: correct if a command is included

turns.content: what radio's the float using again?
HC-12, 433 MHz. It transmits the depth-over-time record after recovery, not during the dive - no line to the surface.

author_note: Generated from a hand-authored method template + profile-grounded scenario (deterministic, no tokens).
rubric: correct if a command is included

turns.content: what's neutral pulse for the ESCs?
1500 µs. Just remember the usable range is 1228-1772, not the old 1100-1900 in some comments - and check neutral after warm-up, the PCA9685 drifts.

turns.content: do I really need to keep D+/D- twisted through the splice?

author_note: Generated from a hand-authored method template + profile-grounded scenario (deterministic, no tokens).
rubric: correct if a command is included

turns.content: quick one - epoxy the camera connector now or test first?
Test first, always. Enumerate and stream through the fully assembled cable before any epoxy - once it's potted a bad D+/D- joint is unfixable. Confirm, then pot.

author_note: Generated from a hand-authored method template + profile-grounded scenario (deterministic, no tokens).
rubric: correct if a command is included

turns.content: does the float need a fuse?
Yes - a 3 A blade fuse on the Tenergy 12 V pack. Cheap protection for a sealed can you don't want thermal-running underwater.

author_note: Generated from a hand-authored method template + profile-grounded scenario (deterministic, no tokens).
rubric: correct if a command is included

turns.content: quick one - is 100 Mbps actually enough for camera plus control?
Yes, comfortably - MJPEG camera plus the control packets are nowhere near 100 Mbps. The cap is for EMI stability, not bandwidth; you're not giving anything up.

author_note: Generated from a hand-authored method template + profile-grounded scenario (deterministic, no tokens).
rubric: correct if a command is included

turns.content: which subnet is the wired link on?
192.168.8.x, static, no gateway - that's what keeps it isolated from the internet. The Pi's 192.168.8.1 on eth0.

author_note: Generated from a hand-authored method template + profile-grounded scenario (deterministic, no tokens).
rubric: correct if a command is included

turns.content: quick one - can I disable the router's wifi to stop it routing?
No - you'd kill the AP with it. Demote it instead: turn off repeater/uplink so it stops routing but stays a local hub. Piloting rides the Pi's wired link regardless.

UDP 5005. (Camera's TCP 5000/5001, launcher's 5010.)

author_note: Generated from a hand-authored method template + profile-grounded scenario (deterministic, no tokens).
rubric: correct if a command is included

turns.content: what runs the float's ballast pump?
A DRV8833 H-bridge - it runs the peristaltic pump both directions for ballast in and out. On the Tenergy 12 V pack, fused at 3 A.

author_note: Generated from a hand-authored method template + profile-grounded scenario (deterministic, no tokens).
rubric: correct if a command is included

turns.content: float has to be under what height again?
Under 1 m tall and under 18 cm diameter, no line to the surface. Two profiles to 2.5 m, holds, then it transmits after recovery.

author_note: Generated from a hand-authored method template + profile-grounded scenario (deterministic, no tokens).
rubric: correct if a command is included

turns.content: quick one - gigabit vs 100 on the tether, quick call?
100. Gigabit flaps under motor EMI and drops comms mid-run; you don't need the bandwidth. `ethtool -s eth0 speed 100 duplex full autoneg off`.

author_note: Generated from a hand-authored method template + profile-grounded scenario (deterministic, no tokens).
rubric: correct if a command is included

turns.content: quick one - max depth for the profile task?
2.5 m, ±33 cm on the hold, then up to 40 cm and hold again. Two profiles, 30 s holds.

author_note: Generated from a hand-authored method template + profile-grounded scenario (deterministic, no tokens).
rubric: correct if a command is included

turns.content: quick one - do I actually need a star ground?
For the EMI you're fighting, yes - a single ground point stops the return-current loops that couple thruster noise into I2C and USB. It's the cheap half of the conducted-noise fix, before cap banks.

author_note: Generated from a hand-authored method template + profile-grounded scenario (deterministic, no tokens).
rubric: correct if a command is included

turns.content: epoxy the camera connector now or test first?
Test first, always. Enumerate and stream through the fully assembled cable before any epoxy - once it's potted a bad D+/D- joint is unfixable. Confirm, then pot.

author_note: Generated from a hand-authored method template + profile-grounded scenario (deterministic, no tokens).
rubric: correct if a command is included

turns.content: is gigabit ever ok on the tether, or never?
Never in the water under motor load - it flaps on EMI. On the bench with motors off it'll negotiate fine, which is exactly what fools you. Cap it 100 for anything real.

author_note: Generated from a hand-authored method template + profile-grounded scenario (deterministic, no tokens).
rubric: correct if a command is included

turns.content: which pressure sensor are we on?
Bar30 (MS5837) over I2C. It's the depth source for the float's profile - watch for jumpy reads, it's usually noise/read-timing, not the sensor.
